items.rationale: Sees ZK as a foundational breakthrough that will rebuild the web3 stack, enabling both scaling (rollups) and privacy/encryption; sustained personal obsession from Aleo/zkSync through Aztec.

citations.quote: Loved discussing zk-proofs with @laurashin and @danboneh on the @unchained_pod. We could have gone on for hours, so much to cover.
Zero Knowledge Proofs will completely transform the web3 tech stack in the coming years. Aleo is at the forefront of that transformation.

items.rationale: Believes most chains are fully transparent which compromises users; views privacy/encryption as essential for mainstream adoption and as opening a whole new region of the design space.
items.age_note: Theme persists consistently 2021-2022 (Nym, IronFish) into most recent statements (Aztec, Dec 2022).

citations.quote: Encryption & privacy will be essential for blockchains to broaden their utility and gain widespread adoption.
Privacy unlocks a whole new region of the design space

items.rationale: Excited by crypto mechanisms incentivizing crowdsourced data, ML models, and compute ('collective AI'); treats the AI-token design space as large and not to be dismissed.
items.age_note: Early interest (Kosen Labs, Nov 2021) reaffirmed in most recent window (Jan-Feb 2023).

citations.quote: AI + crypto = collective AI. Crypto mechanisms can incentivize the crowdsourcing of better data sets, machine learning models, and compute.
It's a very large design space. Hard to dismiss it outright.

items.rationale: Frames modern blockchains as a new kind of computer needing an OS/dev platform; wants innovation at every layer, not just core protocol and smart contracts.

citations.quote: We need innovation at every level of the stack, not just the core protocol and smart contract layers. NEAR is leading the way
@AlchemyPlatform is building the operating system and developer platform for web3.

items.rationale: Views Ethereum throughput as a persistent bottleneck and ZK-rollups as the breakthrough to scale without sacrificing security, decentralization, or composability.
items.age_note: Core thesis articulated heavily Nov 2021 (zkSync); not restated verbatim recently but consistent with ongoing ZK enthusiasm.

citations.quote: @zkSync uses zk-SNARKS, a cryptographic innovation, to scale Ethereum without sacrificing security, decentralization, or composability.

items.rationale: Sees DAOs as a new internet-native form of economic organization enabling group coordination far cheaper and faster than traditional legal structures.

citations.quote: coordination mechanisms that are 10,000x cheaper, faster, and more powerful than traditional legal structures
DAOs like PleasrDAO are foundational to web3. They are a new form of economic organization native to the Internet

items.rationale: Believes web2's structural problems are worst for creators (especially music); web3 tools remove middlemen and let artists own their smart contracts and monetize directly.

citations.quote: The music industry is broken. It is the category for which the structural problems of web2 are at their very worst.
Shibuya will become infrastructure to support creatives, artists, and storytellers.

items.rationale: Points to Helium as proof that crypto incentives can bootstrap real-world networks faster than centralized incumbents, showing crypto's usefulness extends far beyond currencies.

citations.quote: Helium's use of crypto incentives spurred the world's fastest-growing wireless network... a perfect example of how crypto applications go well beyond crypto-currencies.

items.rationale: Backs building a more composable and interoperable future connecting many chains, though paired with strong concern about doing it securely.

citations.quote: we are beyond excited to be partnering with... the entire @LayerZero_Labs team to build a more composable and interoperable future for web3

items.rationale: Average users cannot tell what they are signing; lacking standards, schemas and UX for flagging malicious transactions, this is a major barrier and attack surface.

citations.quote: it's all but impossible for the avg user to know what the hell they're signing with today's tools.

items.rationale: Bridges are a repeated exploit vector; he publicly opposed deploying Uniswap on BNB Chain via Wormhole, signaling caution on bridge trust assumptions even while bullish on interoperability.
items.age_note: Most recent governance stance (Feb 2023) follows the Nomad bridge attack coverage (Aug 2022).

citations.quote: Our rationale for opposing @Uniswap Governance Proposal 31 to deploy Uniswap on BNB Chain using Wormhole

items.rationale: Worries society is increasingly vicious toward those building new tech (crypto, AI, etc.) and that top-down government science funding crowds out innovation; wants bottom-up market-driven funding.

citations.quote: I'm constantly shocked by how vicious society can be toward people who are trying to build something new. Be it AI, crypto, rockets, nuclear, anything...
Increasing government funding of science is often net negative—worse than ineffective—slowing down innovation.

items.rationale: Sees the PoS transition as making Ethereum a far superior chain—more secure, ~100x more energy efficient, with finality that lays groundwork for rollups and better scaling.
items.age_note: Stated around The Merge (Sep 2022); never reversed, consistent with continued ZK/L2-on-Ethereum focus.

citations.quote: Once it's done, I believe Ethereum will be a far superior blockchain than it was before.
After the merge, Ethereum will be 100x+ more energy-efficient than it is today.

risk_regime.rationale: Relentlessly bullish builder-investor: declared a 'golden era of web3,' raised Crypto Fund 4, and kept leading deals through the 2022 downturn. His concerns are about specific risks (bridges, wallet UX) and culture, not a pullback in conviction. Frames frontier risk as the price of venturing out, not a reason to retreat.

notes: Ali Yahya is an a16z crypto partner whose personal lens is deeply technical and infrastructure-first; he rarely talks tradeable tokens or price, so token signal is thin (ETH is the clearest, inferred from his Merge enthusiasm). His strongest and most durable personal convictions are zero-knowledge proofs and privacy/encryption—reaffirmed in the most recent statements (Mar 2023 zk-proofs podcast). AI+crypto is a rising recent interest. Concerns are targeted (wallet signing safety, bridge security) rather than macro/market; he also voices a broader cultural concern about hostility toward builders and top-down science funding. No major reversals detected across the corpus—his thesis is remarkably consistent over 18 months, so most entries are grounded or persisted rather than contradicted.
